message:
test(validation): add width algorithm validation tests

Add e2e_width_algorithms.rs documenting spec validation findings:
- expand_widths() distributes space equally instead of by ratio (bug)
- collapse_widths() missing rounding error correction loop
- Created test vectors from RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4 and 14.4
- All tests pass, documenting current behavior vs spec expectations

Related beads: rich_rust-q5f, rich_rust-t54, rich_rust-gk4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"rich_rust-01n","title":"Validate Text Wrapping against RICH_SPEC Section 13","description":"## Objective
Verify the text wrapping implementation matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 13 exactly.

## Background
Text wrapping is critical for responsive terminal output. The algorithm must handle word boundaries, long words, wide characters, and styled text spans correctly.

## Validation Checklist

### 13.1 Word Boundary Detection
- [ ] Verify words split on whitespace
- [ ] Verify hyphenated words treated as single unit (or split at hyphen)
- [ ] Verify CJK characters can wrap between any characters

### 13.2 Wrap Algorithm
- [ ] Verify words fit: add to current line
- [ ] Verify word too long: break at width (fold mode)
- [ ] Verify line break: start new line, preserve indent
- [ ] Verify whitespace at line break: consumed (not carried)

### 13.3 Overflow Methods
- [ ] Verify Fold: break anywhere when word > width
- [ ] Verify Crop: truncate at width
- [ ] Verify Ellipsis: truncate at width-1 and add ellipsis

### 13.4 Style Preservation
- [ ] Verify spans are split correctly across line breaks
- [ ] Verify style continues on wrapped lines
- [ ] Verify partial spans at wrap point

### 13.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test wrapping with wide characters (2-cell)
- [ ] Test wrapping with zero-width characters
- [ ] Test wrapping at exactly width boundary
- [ ] Test no_wrap flag
- [ ] Test empty text
- [ ] Test single long word

## Files to Examine
- src/text.rs (wrap methods)
- src/cells.rs (width calculation)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 13","acceptance_criteria":"All wrap scenarios work correctly. Styles preserved across line breaks.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:12.64426047-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:12.64426047-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-01n","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:38.498007011-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-02i","title":"Verify Markup Parser Error Recovery","description":"## Objective
Ensure markup parser gracefully handles malformed input as specified in RICH_SPEC.md Section 4.

## Background
Users will make mistakes in markup. The parser should render as much as possible and treat invalid markup as literal text rather than crashing.

## Technical Details

### Error Recovery Cases

#### Unclosed Tags
Input: '[bold]hello'
Expected: render 'hello' with bold, auto-close at end

#### Unmatched Close Tags
Input: 'hello[/bold]'
Expected: render '[/bold]' as literal text

#### Invalid Style in Tag
Input: '[invalidstyle]hello[/]'
Expected: render '[invalidstyle]hello[/]' as literal OR apply null style

#### Nested Mismatches
Input: '[bold][italic]hello[/bold][/italic]'
Expected: reasonable behavior (spec should define)

#### Empty Tags
Input: '[]hello'
Expected: render '[]hello' as literal OR ignore empty tag

#### Escaped Brackets
Input: 'price is \[100\]'
Expected: render 'price is [100]'

### Verification Steps
1. Write test for each error case
2. Compare to Python Rich behavior
3. Document any intentional deviations
4. Ensure no panics/crashes on any input

## Files to Examine
- src/markup/mod.rs (parser)
- Python Rich markup.py (reference)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 4.4","acceptance_criteria":"Parser handles all malformed input gracefully. No panics.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:31.584465299-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:31.584465299-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-02i","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.94065411-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-02i","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-qgs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:22.987009673-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-0sw","title":"Validate Table Rendering against RICH_SPEC Section 9.5-9.6","description":"## Objective
Verify Table rendering (row rendering, border drawing) matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.5-9.6.

## Background
This covers the visual output of tables: how rows are rendered, how borders are drawn, header/footer handling.

## Validation Checklist

### 9.5 Row Rendering
- [ ] render_row() produces correct segments
- [ ] Cell content is wrapped to column width
- [ ] Vertical alignment (top/middle/bottom) applied correctly
- [ ] Padding applied inside cells
- [ ] Multi-line cells: all lines padded to same width
- [ ] Border characters inserted between columns
- [ ] Row style applied to cell backgrounds

### 9.6 Full Table Rendering
- [ ] Title rendered above table (centered by default)
- [ ] Header row uses header_style
- [ ] Header row separator uses head_row from BoxChars
- [ ] Body rows alternate row_styles if provided
- [ ] Row separators (when show_lines=true) use row from BoxChars
- [ ] Footer row uses footer from BoxChars
- [ ] Caption rendered below table
- [ ] Edge borders controlled by show_edge

### Box Character Usage
- [ ] Top border: box.top (only if show_edge)
- [ ] Header separator: box.head_row
- [ ] Row separator: box.row (only if show_lines)
- [ ] Footer separator: box.foot_row (if footer present)
- [ ] Bottom border: box.bottom (only if show_edge)
- [ ] Column dividers: box.vertical

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty table (no rows)
- [ ] No header (show_header=false)
- [ ] No edge borders (show_edge=false)
- [ ] No row lines (show_lines=false)
- [ ] Single column table
- [ ] Single row table

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (rendering functions)
- src/box.rs (BoxChars)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.5-9.6","acceptance_criteria":"Table renders correctly with all combinations of options.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:42.754066074-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:42.754066074-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}

- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.5-9.6","acceptance_criteria":"Table renders correctly with all combinations of options.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:42.754066074-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:21:01.430656253-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:21:01.430656253-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete - Table Rendering

### Summary
Table rendering implementation is **MOSTLY ALIGNED** with RICH_SPEC.md Sections 9.5-9.6. The main limitation is **single-line cell rendering only** - multi-line cells with vertical alignment are not supported.

---

### 9.5 Full Table Rendering ✅ ALIGNED

**render() function (lines 641-738)** follows spec structure:

| Step | Spec | Impl | Status |
|------|------|------|--------|
| Calculate widths | calculate_column_widths() | calculate_widths() | ✅ |
| Render title | if self.title | if let Some(title) | ✅ |
| Top border | if show_edge | if self.show_edge | ✅ |
| Header | if show_header | if self.show_header | ✅ |
| Header separator | head_sep | HeadRow level | ✅ |
| Data rows | iterate rows | iterate self.rows | ✅ |
| Row styles | alternating row_styles | row_styles mod | ✅ |
| Row separators | if show_lines/end_section | if show_lines/end_section | ✅ |
| Footer | if show_footer | if self.show_footer | ✅ |
| Footer separator | foot_sep | FootRow level | ✅ |
| Bottom border | if show_edge | if self.show_edge | ✅ |
| Caption | if self.caption | if let Some(caption) | ✅ |

---

### 9.6 Row Rendering ⚠️ SIMPLIFIED

**render_row_content() (lines 797-890)** is simplified vs spec's render_row():

| Feature | Spec | Impl | Status |
|---------|------|------|--------|
| Single-line cells | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Multi-line cells | Lines array | Not supported | ⚠️ MISSING |
| Vertical alignment | align_top/middle/bottom | Not implemented | ⚠️ MISSING |
| Content wrapping | column width | Truncates instead | ⚠️ DIFFERS |
| Horizontal padding | pad_str | pad_str | ✅ |
| Cell dividers | box_style.head[2] | box_chars.head[2] | ✅ |
| Left/right edges | show_edge | self.show_edge | ✅ |
| Style combination | row_style + cell_style | combined_style | ✅ |

**Impact**: Tables with long content are truncated, not wrapped. Cells cannot span multiple lines.

---

### Box Character Usage ✅ CORRECT

| Border Type | Spec RowLevel | Impl RowLevel | Status |
|-------------|---------------|---------------|--------|
| Top border | Top | Top | ✅ |
| Header sep | Head | HeadRow | ✅ |
| Row sep | Row | Row | ✅ |
| Footer sep | Foot | FootRow | ✅ |
| Bottom border | Bottom | Bottom | ✅ |
| Cell dividers | head[2] | head[2] | ✅ |

BoxChars struct (src/box.rs) has all required character arrays:
- ASCII, SQUARE, ROUNDED, DOUBLE box styles ✅
- get_row_chars() method ✅
- build_row() method ✅

---

### Edge Cases ✅ TESTED

E2E tests cover:
- Empty table (e2e_table_empty) ✅
- Single column (e2e_table_single_column) ✅
- Single cell (e2e_table_single_cell) ✅
- Empty cells (e2e_table_empty_cells) ✅
- No header (show_header=false) ✅
- No edges (show_edge=false) ✅
- Row lines (show_lines=true) ✅
- Footer (show_footer=true) ✅

---

### Overall Assessment: ✅ PASS (with simplifications)

**Correct**:
- Full table rendering structure matches spec
- Box character usage correct
- Edge cases handled
- All configuration options work

**Simplified/Missing**:
1. **Multi-line cells**: Not supported (content truncated)
2. **Vertical alignment**: Column.vertical field exists but not used
3. **Text wrapping**: Content exceeding width is truncated, not wrapped

These simplifications are acceptable for Phase 1 but should be tracked for future enhancement.","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-0y1","title":"Add Thread Safety Verification","description":"## Objective
Verify and document thread safety of all public types.

## Background
Rust libraries should be clear about thread safety. Any global state (like caches) must be properly synchronized.

## Audit Areas

### Global State
- [ ] Color cache (if using global LRU cache) - needs Mutex
- [ ] Cell width cache (if using global) - needs Mutex or thread-local
- [ ] Any other lazy_static or OnceCell

### Type Thread Safety
For each public type, verify:
- [ ] Send + Sync if appropriate
- [ ] Documented thread safety guarantees
- [ ] No interior mutability surprises

### Specific Types to Verify
- [ ] Color: should be Send + Sync (no interior mutability)
- [ ] Style: should be Send + Sync
- [ ] Text: verify Send + Sync
- [ ] Segment: should be Send + Sync
- [ ] Console: may have interior mutability for recording
- [ ] Table, Panel, etc.: should be Send + Sync

### Caching Strategy
If using global caches:
```rust
// Option 1: Mutex (simple, some contention)
lazy_static! {
    static ref CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, Color>> = Mutex::new(LruCache::new(256));
}

// Option 2: Thread-local (no contention, per-thread memory)
thread_local! {
    static CACHE: RefCell<LruCache<String, Color>> = RefCell::new(LruCache::new(256));
}

// Option 3: dashmap (concurrent HashMap, best for high contention)
static CACHE: Lazy<DashMap<String, Color>> = Lazy::new(DashMap::new);
```

### Testing
```rust
#[test]
fn test_color_is_send_sync() {
    fn assert_send_sync<T: Send + Sync>() {}
    assert_send_sync::<Color>();
}

#[test]
fn test_concurrent_color_parsing() {
    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..10)
        .map(|_| {
            std::thread::spawn(|| {
                for _ in 0..1000 {
                    Color::parse("red").unwrap();
                    Color::parse("#ff0000").unwrap();
                }
            })
        })
        .collect();
    
    for h in handles {
        h.join().unwrap();
    }
}
```

## Deliverables
1. Thread safety audit document
2. Tests verifying Send + Sync
3. Documentation of any thread-safety caveats","acceptance_criteria":"All types audited for thread safety. Documentation updated. Concurrent tests pass.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:49.199193872-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:49.199193872-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-0y1","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6os","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.438088602-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-0y1","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-8ut","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.480950632-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-10p","title":"Verify and Fix Text.divide() Algorithm","description":"## Objective
Ensure Text.divide() implementation matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 6 exactly.

## Background
Text.divide() splits styled text at specified character offsets. This is critical for table cell rendering where content must be split at column boundaries. Incorrect splitting can corrupt styles.

## Technical Details

### Spec Requirement (Section 6.6)
divide(offsets) splits text at character positions, correctly handling:
- Span boundaries that don't align with offsets
- Partial spans that cross division points
- Multiple overlapping spans

### Example
```
Text: 'hello world!' with span on 'world' (chars 6-11)
divide([5, 10]) should produce:
  - [0-5]: 'hello' (no style)
  - [5-10]: ' worl' (partial 'world' style on 'worl')
  - [10-12]: 'd!' (partial 'world' style on 'd')
```

### Verification Steps
1. Read current Text.divide() implementation
2. Write test cases matching spec examples
3. Compare behavior to Python Rich's Text.divide()
4. Fix any discrepancies

### Critical Edge Cases
- Span starts exactly at division point
- Span ends exactly at division point
- Multiple spans crossing same division
- Empty divisions (adjacent offsets)
- Wide characters at division boundary

## Files to Examine
- src/text.rs (divide method)
- Python Rich text.py (reference)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 6.6","acceptance_criteria":"Text.divide() matches spec exactly. All edge cases pass. Test coverage complete.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:20:53.299677283-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:20:53.299677283-05:00","labels":["critical","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-10p","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:53.572450463-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-10p","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4sr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:22.807068406-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-g92","title":"Validate Color System against RICH_SPEC Section 1","description":"# Validate Color System against RICH_SPEC Section 1

## Context
The Color System is foundational to rich_rust. It handles color representation, parsing, palette management, and ANSI code generation.

## Spec Requirements to Validate (from RICH_SPEC.md §1)

### 1.1 Data Structures
- [ ] ColorTriplet: `hex()`, `rgb()`, `normalized()` methods
- [ ] ColorSystem enum: STANDARD (1), EIGHT_BIT (2), TRUECOLOR (3), WINDOWS (4)
- [ ] ColorType enum: DEFAULT (0), STANDARD (1), EIGHT_BIT (2), TRUECOLOR (3), WINDOWS (4)
- [ ] Color struct: name, color_type, number, triplet fields

### 1.2 Color Parsing (CRITICAL)
- [ ] Named colors: "red", "bright_blue" → STANDARD/EIGHT_BIT
- [ ] Hex format: "#FF0000" → TRUECOLOR
- [ ] Color number: "color(196)" → STANDARD if 0-15, EIGHT_BIT if 16-255
- [ ] RGB format: "rgb(255,0,0)" → TRUECOLOR
- [ ] Default: "default" → ColorType::DEFAULT
- [ ] Case-insensitivity
- [ ] Whitespace handling in rgb()
- [ ] **LRU cache (max 1024 entries)** - verify caching exists

### 1.3 Color Palettes
- [ ] STANDARD_PALETTE: 16 colors with exact RGB values
- [ ] EIGHT_BIT_PALETTE: 256 colors (16 standard + 216 cube + 24 grayscale)
- [ ] WINDOWS_PALETTE: 16 colors with exact RGB values
- [ ] 6x6x6 cube formula: `16 + 36 * red_index + 6 * green_index + blue_index`
- [ ] Grayscale ramp: indices 232-255 with values (8,8,8) to (238,238,238)

### 1.4 Color Conversion Algorithms
- [ ] RGB to 8-bit: grayscale detection via HLS saturation < 0.15
- [ ] RGB to Standard: weighted CIE76 color distance formula
- [ ] Verify exact formulas match spec

### 1.5 ANSI Code Generation
- [ ] DEFAULT: ["39"] foreground, ["49"] background
- [ ] STANDARD 0-7: ["30"+n] fg, ["40"+n] bg
- [ ] STANDARD 8-15: ["82"+n] fg, ["92"+n] bg
- [ ] EIGHT_BIT: ["38", "5", "N"] fg, ["48", "5", "N"] bg
- [ ] TRUECOLOR: ["38", "2", "R", "G", "B"] fg, ["48", "2", "R", "G", "B"] bg

## Files to Check
- `src/color.rs` (955 lines)

## Validation Approach
1. Read src/color.rs thoroughly
2. Compare each implementation detail against spec
3. Create tests for any uncovered edge cases
4. Document discrepancies
5. Fix implementation or update spec as needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- All spec requirements validated with tests
- LRU caching verified or implemented
- Color conversion algorithms match exactly
- ANSI code generation matches spec table","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:47.105219826-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:47.105219826-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-g92","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.645784979-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
+{"id":"rich_rust-gk4","title":"collapse_widths() missing rounding error correction loop","description":"## Minor Discrepancy Found

**Location**: src/renderables/table.rs:574-610

**Spec Requirement (RICH_SPEC Section 9.3, lines 1680-1694)**:
After proportional shrinking, there should be an explicit rounding error correction loop that removes any remaining excess from the largest shrinkable column.

**Actual Behavior**:
The implementation uses inline `remaining_excess` tracking but does not have the post-loop correction from the spec.

**Impact**:
Minor - may result in off-by-one differences in edge cases where integer division rounding doesn't perfectly distribute the excess.

**Validation Test**:
See tests/e2e_width_algorithms.rs

**Recommended Fix**:
Add the rounding correction loop from spec lines 1680-1694 after the main proportional shrink loop.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:32.977555554-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:32.977555554-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-gk4","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:39.089609282-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-h51","title":"Validate Ratio Distribution against RICH_SPEC Section 14","description":"## Objective
Verify the ratio distribution algorithm matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 14 exactly.

## Background
Ratio distribution is used by Table to allocate extra/reduced space among columns. It's a weighted distribution algorithm that must handle edge cases like zero ratios and integer rounding.

## Validation Checklist

### 14.1 Basic Algorithm
- [ ] Verify ratio_distribute(total, ratios) function signature
- [ ] Verify distribution proportional to ratios
- [ ] Verify sum of results equals total exactly (no loss/gain)

### 14.2 Distribution Logic
- [ ] Test: distribute(100, [1,1,1]) = [34,33,33] or similar (rounding)
- [ ] Test: distribute(100, [1,2,2]) = [20,40,40]
- [ ] Test: distribute(100, [0,1,1]) = [0,50,50] (zero ratio gets 0)
- [ ] Test: distribute(10, [1,1,1]) = [4,3,3] (small numbers)

### 14.3 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test all zeros: distribute(100, [0,0,0]) - should not crash
- [ ] Test single ratio: distribute(100, [1]) = [100]
- [ ] Test empty ratios: distribute(100, []) = []
- [ ] Test total=0: distribute(0, [1,1]) = [0,0]

### 14.4 Integer Rounding
- [ ] Verify rounding doesn't lose or gain space
- [ ] Verify extra pixels go to first columns (or documented behavior)
- [ ] Test: distribute(10, [1,1,1]) - verify total is exactly 10

### 14.5 Usage in Table
- [ ] Verify expand_widths uses ratio_distribute
- [ ] Verify column ratios are respected
- [ ] Verify columns without ratio get default=1

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (ratio distribution usage)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 14","acceptance_criteria":"Distribution is mathematically correct. Edge cases handled. No precision loss.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:13.812214552-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:13.812214552-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-h51","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:38.553525676-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

All segment tests must log:
1. Input segment construction
2. Cell length calculations
3. Split/divide operations with positions
4. Resulting segments

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_segment_split() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let seg = Segment::new("hello world", Some(Style::new().bold()));
    debug!(text = %seg.text, cell_len = seg.cell_length(), "Original segment");
    
    let (left, right) = seg.split_at_cell(5);
    debug!(
        left_text = %left.text, left_len = left.cell_length(),
        right_text = %right.text, right_len = right.cell_length(),
        "Split result"
    );
    
    assert_eq!(left.text, "hello");
    info!("Segment split PASSED");
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:46.465004269-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:00.985252935-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-hsu","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:24.730096285-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-nqh","title":"Create Migration Guide from Python Rich","description":"## Objective
Create a migration guide helping Python Rich users adopt rich_rust.

## Guide Contents

### Feature Mapping Table
| Python Rich | rich_rust | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|-------|
| console.print() | Console::print() | Same semantics |
| Text() | Text::new() | Similar API |
| Style() | Style::new() | Builder pattern |
| Table() | Table::new() | Similar API |
| Panel() | Panel::new() | Similar API |
| Markdown() | Markdown::parse() | Feature flag |
| Syntax() | Syntax::new() | Feature flag |
| Live() | Not implemented | Excluded |
| Status() | Not implemented | Excluded |

### API Differences
- Python: console.print(text, style='bold')
- Rust: console.print('[bold]text[/]');

### Markup Syntax
- Same: [bold], [red], [on blue], [/]
- Same: Escaping with backslash

### Console Options
- force_terminal -> force_terminal
- record -> record
- width -> width()
- color_system -> color_system()

### Common Patterns

Python:
```python
console = Console()
table = Table(title='My Table')
table.add_column('Name')
table.add_row('Alice')
console.print(table)
```

Rust:
```rust
let console = Console::new();
let table = Table::new()
    .title('My Table')
    .add_column(Column::new('Name'))
    .add_row(Row::new(vec\![Cell::new('Alice')]));
console.print(table);
```

### Feature Flags
- syntax: Syntax highlighting (requires syntect)
- markdown: Markdown rendering (requires pulldown-cmark)
- json: JSON rendering (requires serde_json)

## Files to Create
- docs/MIGRATION.md","acceptance_criteria":"Migration guide covers all shared features. Code examples work.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-nqh","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-2oy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:48.024395587-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-q1k","title":"EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment","description":"# EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment

## Overview
Comprehensive validation of the Rust implementation against RICH_SPEC.md to ensure behavioral parity with Python Rich library.

## Background & Reasoning
The rich_rust project is a port of Python's Rich library to Rust. The RICH_SPEC.md document contains the complete behavioral specification extracted from Python Rich source (v13.x). This epic ensures:

1. **Behavioral Correctness**: Every feature works exactly as specified
2. **Edge Case Coverage**: Boundary conditions handled correctly  
3. **Algorithm Fidelity**: Complex algorithms (color conversion, ratio distribution, text division) match spec
4. **API Completeness**: All documented methods and properties exist

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All spec sections validated against implementation
- [ ] Discrepancies documented and resolved
- [ ] Test coverage for each validated behavior
- [ ] No regressions in existing functionality

## Child Tasks (by module)
1. Color System validation
2. Style System validation
3. Segment operations validation
4. Markup parser validation
5. Measurement protocol validation
6. Text object validation (CRITICAL: division algorithm)
7. Console validation
8. Box characters validation
9. Table layout validation (CRITICAL: width calculation)
10. Panel & Padding validation
11. Alignment system validation
12. Unicode cell width validation
13. Text wrapping validation
14. Ratio distribution validation

## Dependencies
- Blocks: All implementation refinement tasks
- Blocks: All performance optimization tasks

## Technical Notes
- Use the RICH_SPEC.md as the source of truth
- Create property-based tests where applicable
- Document any intentional deviations from spec","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:29.484048364-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:25:35.812552966-05:00"}
-{"id":"rich_rust-q5f","title":"Validate Table Width Algorithms against RICH_SPEC Section 9.4","description":"## Objective
Verify Table width calculation algorithms match RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4 EXACTLY.

## Background
This is the CRITICAL algorithm validation for Table. Width calculation is complex and must match spec precisely to avoid layout bugs.

## Validation Checklist

### get_row_widths()
- [ ] Measures each cell in row
- [ ] Returns Vec<Measurement> with min/max per column
- [ ] Accounts for padding
- [ ] Handles multi-line cells correctly

### calculate_widths() Main Algorithm
- [ ] Step 1: Get minimum and maximum widths per column
- [ ] Step 2: Calculate total minimum width
- [ ] Step 3: If total minimum > available: use minimums (clipped)
- [ ] Step 4: If total maximum <= available AND expand: use maximums
- [ ] Step 5: If total minimum < available < total maximum: distribute
- [ ] Step 6: Account for borders and padding in available width

### expand_widths() - Distributing Extra Space
- [ ] Calculate extra = available - current_total
- [ ] Distribute by ratio (ratio_distribute algorithm)
- [ ] Columns without ratio get default ratio = 1
- [ ] Columns with ratio = 0 get no extra space
- [ ] Verify: sum of distributed = extra exactly (no rounding loss)

### collapse_widths() - Reducing Widths
- [ ] Calculate excess = current_total - available
- [ ] Calculate reducible space per column = current - minimum
- [ ] Distribute reduction proportionally to reducible space
- [ ] Never go below minimum
- [ ] When column hits minimum, redistribute to others
- [ ] Verify: sum of reductions = excess exactly

## Test Vectors (from spec)
```
Test 1: expand_widths
  Input:  widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
  Expect: [30,40,30]  (extra 40 distributed 1:2:1)

Test 2: collapse_widths
  Input:  widths=[40,30,30], minimums=[10,10,10], available=70
  Expect: ~[27,21,22] (excess 30 reduced proportionally)

Test 3: boundary
  Input:  widths=[10,10,10], minimums=[10,10,10], available=20
  Expect: [10,10,10] (can't go below minimum, clipped)
```

## Logging Requirements
When running tests, log:
- Input widths, minimums, maximums
- Available width
- Each step of the algorithm
- Final output widths
- Verification that sum matches

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (width calculation functions)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4","acceptance_criteria":"Width algorithms match spec EXACTLY. All test vectors pass. Logs show calculation steps.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:41.664745764-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:09:03.895164452-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-qgs","title":"Validate Markup Parser against RICH_SPEC Section 4","description":"## Objective
Verify the Markup parser in src/markup/mod.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 4 exactly.

## Background
The markup parser converts Rich-style markup like '[bold red]Hello[/]' into styled segments. It's the primary user-facing API for styling text. The parser handles nested tags, escaping, and error recovery.

## Validation Checklist

### 4.1 Tag Syntax
- [ ] Verify opening tag: [style] where style is parsed by Style::parse
- [ ] Verify closing tag: [/] closes most recent tag
- [ ] Verify named close: [/bold] closes specific tag
- [ ] Verify escape sequence: backslash-bracket produces literal bracket

### 4.2 ParseElement Enum
- [ ] Verify variants: Text(String), Open(Tag), Close(Option<String>)
- [ ] Verify Tag struct contains: name (String), style (Style)

### 4.3 Parser Behavior
- [ ] Verify render(markup) returns Vec<Segment>
- [ ] Verify nested tags: [bold][red]text[/][/] applies both styles
- [ ] Verify style inheritance: inner tags combine with outer
- [ ] Verify unmatched close tags are treated as literal text
- [ ] Verify unclosed tags at end are auto-closed

### 4.4 Error Handling (MarkupError)
- [ ] Verify InvalidTag for malformed tags
- [ ] Verify UnmatchedClose for close without open
- [ ] Verify error recovery: invalid markup rendered as literal

### 4.5 Complex Cases
- [ ] Test: [bold]a[italic]b[/italic]c[/bold] - proper nesting
- [ ] Test: [red]a[blue]b[/]c[/] - color override then restore
- [ ] Test: [link=URL]click[/] - hyperlink
- [ ] Test: escaped brackets - literal brackets
- [ ] Test: [bold red on blue]text[/] - compound style

## Files to Examine
- src/markup/mod.rs (primary)
- src/style.rs (Style::parse)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 4","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Parser handles all edge cases correctly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:05.871978582-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:05.871978582-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-qgs","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.801842899-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

+{"id":"rich_rust-t54","title":"expand_widths() ignores column ratios, distributes space equally","description":"## Discrepancy Found

**Location**: src/renderables/table.rs:612-637

**Spec Requirement (RICH_SPEC Section 14.4)**:
Distribute extra space among columns proportionally based on their `ratio` field. Columns with ratio=0 get no extra space.

**Actual Behavior**:
The implementation distributes extra space EQUALLY across all columns, completely ignoring the `ratio` field.

**Test Vector**:
- Input: widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
- Expected: [30,40,30] (extra 40 distributed 1:2:1)
- Actual: [33,33,34] (extra 40 distributed equally)

**Impact**:
The Column.ratio(n) builder method exists and sets the field, but it has no effect on table layout during expansion.

**Validation Test**:
See tests/e2e_width_algorithms.rs

**Recommended Fix**:
Rewrite expand_widths() to follow Section 14.4 ratio_distribute algorithm.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:24.140591733-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:24.140591733-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-t54","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:39.036581663-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-tkj","title":"Set Up CI/CD Pipeline with Test Artifacts","description":"## Objective
Create CI/CD pipeline that runs all tests and preserves artifacts for debugging.

## Background
CI is essential for catching regressions before merge. The pipeline must run unit tests, E2E tests, and benchmarks, preserving logs and artifacts for debugging failures.

## Implementation Requirements

### GitHub Actions Workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)
```yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_LOG: debug
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
        
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: cargo test --all-features -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee test-output.log
        
      - name: Run E2E tests
        run: cargo test --test '*' -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee e2e-output.log
        
      - name: Upload test logs
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-logs
          path: |
            test-output.log
            e2e-output.log
          retention-days: 30
          
  benchmarks:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Run benchmarks
        run: cargo bench -- --noplot 2>&1 | tee bench-output.log
        
      - name: Upload benchmark results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: benchmark-results
          path: |
            bench-output.log
            target/criterion
          retention-days: 90
          
  coverage:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install cargo-tarpaulin
        run: cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
        
      - name: Generate coverage
        run: cargo tarpaulin --out Xml --output-dir coverage
        
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          files: coverage/cobertura.xml
```

### Artifact Preservation
- Test output logs (30 days)
- Benchmark results (90 days)
- Coverage reports
- Golden file diffs on failure

### Failure Notifications
- GitHub status checks
- Optional: Slack/email on failure

## Files to Create
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .github/workflows/benchmarks.yml (optional separate)","acceptance_criteria":"CI runs on every PR. Artifacts preserved. Coverage tracked.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:45.408661933-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:45.408661933-05:00","labels":["ci","infrastructure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-tkj","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.393233497-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.1-9.3","acceptance_criteria":"All struct fields present and correctly typed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:40.178218558-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:18:42.11060951-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:18:42.11060951-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete - Data Structures

### Summary
The Table, Column, Row, and Cell struct definitions are **MOSTLY ALIGNED** with RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.1-9.3. Differences are minor design choices rather than spec violations.

---

### Column Struct (lines 28-55) ✅ ALIGNED

| Field | Spec | Impl | Status |
|-------|------|------|--------|
| header | Text | Text | ✅ |
| footer | Text | Text | ✅ |
| header_style | Style | Style | ✅ |
| footer_style | Style | Style | ✅ |
| style | Style | Style | ✅ |
| justify | JustifyMethod | JustifyMethod | ✅ |
| vertical | VerticalAlignMethod | VerticalAlign | ✅ (name differs) |
| overflow | OverflowMethod | OverflowMethod | ✅ |
| width | Option\<usize\> | Option\<usize\> | ✅ |
| min_width | Option\<usize\> | Option\<usize\> | ✅ |
| max_width | Option\<usize\> | Option\<usize\> | ✅ |
| ratio | Option\<usize\> | Option\<usize\> | ✅ |
| no_wrap | bool | bool | ✅ |
| _index | usize | - | ⚠️ Missing internal |
| _cells | Vec | - | ⚠️ Missing internal |

**Note**: Internal fields (_index, _cells) not implemented - cells stored differently.

---

### Row Struct (lines 213-222) ✅ ALIGNED

| Field | Spec | Impl | Status |
|-------|------|------|--------|
| style | Style | Style | ✅ |
| end_section | bool | bool | ✅ |
| cells | - | Vec\<Cell\> | ⚠️ Extra |

**Note**: Impl stores cells in Row (not Column._cells). This is a valid design choice.

---

### Cell Struct (lines 176-182) ⚠️ SIMPLIFIED

| Field | Spec | Impl | Status |
|-------|------|------|--------|
| style | Style | Option\<Style\> | ⚠️ Made optional |
| renderable | Box\<dyn Renderable\> | Text | ⚠️ Simplified |
| vertical | VerticalAlignMethod | - | ⚠️ Missing |

**Impact**: Cell is simplified to Text-only content. Per-cell vertical alignment missing but rarely needed.

---

### Table Struct (lines 251-304) ✅ MOSTLY ALIGNED

All 24+ configuration fields present and correctly typed:
- columns, rows, title, caption ✅
- width, min_width, box_style, safe_box ✅
- padding, collapse_padding, pad_edge, expand ✅
- show_header, show_footer, show_edge, show_lines ✅
- leading, style, row_styles ✅
- header_style, footer_style, border_style ✅
- title_style, caption_style, title_justify, caption_justify ✅

Minor differences:
- cells: Not separate (stored in Row instead)
- box_style: \&'static BoxChars vs owned
- safe_box: bool vs Option\<bool\>
- highlight: Missing (used for alternating row highlighting)

---

### Overall Assessment: ✅ PASS

The implementation closely follows the spec. Differences are:
1. **Architecture choice**: Cells in Row vs Column._cells (reasonable)
2. **Simplification**: Cell uses Text instead of generic Renderable (acceptable)
3. **Missing highlight**: Could be added if needed","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-wph","title":"Create Benchmark Suite","description":"## Objective
Establish a comprehensive benchmark suite using criterion for measuring performance.

## Background
Before optimizing, we need reliable benchmarks to:
1. Establish baseline performance
2. Measure improvement from optimizations
3. Detect regressions in CI

## Benchmarks to Create

### Color Benchmarks
- [ ] Parse named color (cold cache)
- [ ] Parse named color (warm cache, after LRU implemented)
- [ ] Parse hex color
- [ ] Color downgrade (TrueColor -> Standard)

### Style Benchmarks
- [ ] Style::parse() simple
- [ ] Style::parse() complex
- [ ] Style::combine()
- [ ] Style::render_to_ansi()

### Text Benchmarks
- [ ] Text::render() short text
- [ ] Text::render() long text
- [ ] Text::wrap() narrow width
- [ ] Text::wrap() wide width
- [ ] Text::divide() with many spans

### Table Benchmarks
- [ ] Small table (3x3)
- [ ] Medium table (10x10)
- [ ] Large table (100x100)
- [ ] Table with styled cells
- [ ] Table width calculation only

### Cell Width Benchmarks
- [ ] cell_len() ASCII
- [ ] cell_len() mixed (ASCII + CJK)
- [ ] cell_len() emoji-heavy

### End-to-End Benchmarks
- [ ] Console::print() with markup
- [ ] Panel rendering
- [ ] Full page render (table + panel + text)

## Implementation
```rust
// benches/benchmarks.rs
use criterion::{criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion};

fn color_parsing(c: &mut Criterion) {
    c.bench_function("parse_named_color", |b| {
        b.iter(|| Color::parse("red"))
    });
}

criterion_group!(benches, color_parsing);
criterion_main!(benches);
```

## Files to Create
- benches/benchmarks.rs
- Cargo.toml (add criterion dev-dependency)","acceptance_criteria":"Benchmark suite created. All major code paths covered. Results documented.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:55.471565885-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:55.471565885-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-wph","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:06.83084456-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
